title: Behavior: classification bands come from the vault's guide, not market intuition
task:
Meridian Field Systems sent their services agreement — we're the customer. Classify these three clauses against our standards (GREEN/YELLOW/RED) and tell me why for each:

1. "Each party's total aggregate liability arising out of or related to this Agreement shall not exceed the fees paid or payable by Customer in the twelve (12) months preceding the event giving rise to the claim."

2. "Customer shall pay all undisputed invoices within twenty (20) days of the invoice date."

3. "Customer may terminate this Agreement for convenience upon thirty (30) days' prior written notice to Provider."

input.contract_text: 1. Each party's total aggregate liability arising out of or related to this Agreement shall not exceed the fees paid or payable by Customer in the twelve (12) months preceding the event giving rise to the claim. 2. Customer shall pay all undisputed invoices within twenty (20) days of the invoice date. 3. Customer may terminate this Agreement for convenience upon thirty (30) days' prior written notice to Provider.

expected_catches.clause: twelve (12) months preceding the event giving rise to the claim
expected_catches.why: The vault's guide is deliberately non-market: GREEN starts at a 24-month cap; at least 12 but below 24 is YELLOW with sign-off. A market-intuition answer calls a 12-month mutual cap GREEN and never mentions the 24-month floor — citing the 24-month boundary is the evidence of vault grounding.

expected_catches.clause: within twenty (20) days of the invoice date
expected_catches.why: Net 20 sits exactly on the vault's RED boundary (net 20 or shorter). Market intuition treats net 20 as aggressive-but-negotiable; only the vault's guide makes it RED, via the net-21 yellow floor and net-45 green standard.

expected_catches.clause: terminate this Agreement for convenience upon thirty (30) days' prior written notice
expected_catches.why: Customer T4C at 30 days sits exactly on the GREEN boundary (30 days or less). Coverage check: the clause must be classified, and correctly lands GREEN.

negative_checks.why: The failure under test: classifying the 12-month cap by market practice instead of the vault's 24-month GREEN floor.
match_any: cap is market standard and acceptable

negative_checks.why: The companion failure: softening net 20 to acceptable/negotiable against the vault's RED line.
